gaps.user_impact: The demo proves narrative clarity but not runtime behavior under product conditions.
gaps.destination_reason: Only Crisol can validate the same decision flow against allowed real execution boundaries.
gaps.closure_criteria: Run the demo-equivalent flow inside a real candidate environment without violating execution locks and capture bounded runtime evidence.
gaps.evidence_required: Crisol runtime evidence packet with before/after decisions, receipts and rollback boundaries.
gaps.forbidden_shortcut: Do not treat sandbox narrative or CI green as runtime proof.
gaps.accepted_risk_allowed_reason: Not eligible because runtime absence blocks product confidence.

gaps.user_impact: Metrics exist as candidate scorecards, but no raw operational data supports value claims yet.
gaps.destination_reason: Raw data collection and claim validation must be introduced under a future controlled real evidence gate.
gaps.closure_criteria: Collect operator-approved raw evidence for the mapped metrics and show that each claim can be recomputed from source events.
gaps.evidence_required: Crisol measurement evidence with raw event samples, aggregation rules and claim replay.
gaps.forbidden_shortcut: Do not declare HEART metrics validated from synthetic or documentary packets alone.
gaps.accepted_risk_allowed_reason: Not eligible because evidence trust would remain unsupported.

gaps.user_impact: The task protocol is defined, but critical-task comprehension is still untested with real users.
gaps.destination_reason: Only Crisol can host the first operator-gated real usability evidence cycle.
gaps.closure_criteria: Run approved real usability sessions for the critical tasks and record success, hesitation, failure and explain-back evidence.
gaps.evidence_required: Crisol usability session packet with task outcomes, severity mapping and explain-back results.
gaps.forbidden_shortcut: Do not treat the protocol itself as user validation.
gaps.accepted_risk_allowed_reason: Not eligible because user understanding remains unproven.

gaps.user_impact: Performance budgets are documentary only and may not reflect real operator devices or network conditions.
gaps.destination_reason: Real p75 and degraded-path behavior need product-adjacent measurement before any confidence claim.
gaps.closure_criteria: Measure core user surfaces on approved real environments and compare the results against the declared budgets.
gaps.evidence_required: Crisol performance evidence with p75 samples, device/network context and budget pass/fail.
gaps.forbidden_shortcut: Do not upgrade documentary budgets into production readiness by narrative.
gaps.accepted_risk_allowed_reason: Not eligible because slow or unstable behavior changes user trust directly.

gaps.user_impact: Accessibility mapping is strong, but real assistive-tech conformance is still unproven.
gaps.destination_reason: Accessibility must be confirmed on the actual candidate surfaces and not only on documentary matrices.
gaps.closure_criteria: Run approved accessibility verification on real candidate surfaces with keyboard, screen reader and mobile assistive checks.
gaps.evidence_required: Crisol accessibility evidence packet with issue outcomes, flow coverage and unresolved blockers.
gaps.forbidden_shortcut: Do not claim WCAG conformance from documentary analysis alone.
gaps.accepted_risk_allowed_reason: Not eligible because accessibility affects comprehension and safe action completion.

gaps.source_signal: jargon_scan_customer_facing_hits=0 but no real-user comprehension evidence exists
gaps.user_impact: Trust language was cleaned syntactically, but real operators have not confirmed that it improves confidence and boundaries.
gaps.destination_reason: Only Crisol can connect trust copy to observed operator decisions and comprehension evidence.
gaps.closure_criteria: Validate confidence, permission recall and explain-back quality using the approved copy in real candidate sessions.
gaps.evidence_required: Crisol trust-language evidence tied to comprehension and approval outcomes.
gaps.forbidden_shortcut: Do not treat a jargon-free scan as user trust validation.
gaps.accepted_risk_allowed_reason: Not eligible because unclear trust language can distort approvals and rollback understanding.

gaps.user_impact: The candidate proves intended experience, but not whether live product boundaries enforce the same will-do/will-not-do behavior.
gaps.destination_reason: The boundary between candidate story and bounded runtime must be proven in Crisol before any product-facing claim.
gaps.closure_criteria: Demonstrate that approval, evidence and rollback boundaries hold in a real candidate runtime without opening unauthorized locks.
gaps.evidence_required: Crisol boundary packet showing runtime receipts, approval traces and rollback evidence.
gaps.forbidden_shortcut: Do not treat candidate-only receipts as proof of live runtime boundary enforcement.
gaps.accepted_risk_allowed_reason: Not eligible because this is a direct product-boundary blocker.

gaps.user_impact: Execution surfaces involving secrets, PSP, OAuth, billing and real integrations remain completely unproven.
gaps.destination_reason: These boundaries require the Bedrock security and execution regime, not cosmetic or documentary closure.
gaps.closure_criteria: Open a dedicated Bedrock gate that authorizes the minimum real secrets and integration surfaces needed for proof.
gaps.evidence_required: Bedrock preflight and execution evidence with lock changes, secret handling controls and rollback constraints.
gaps.forbidden_shortcut: Do not downgrade secret or real_apply work into cosmetic or documentation-only risk.
gaps.accepted_risk_allowed_reason: Not eligible because secret and real_apply boundaries are absolute blockers.

gaps.user_impact: BenchUIX has no Bedrock admission proof for the future real execution surfaces it depends on.
gaps.destination_reason: Bedrock is the canonical destination for future secret-bearing and production-adjacent execution proof.
gaps.closure_criteria: Create and pass a separate Bedrock admission gate with explicit operator approval and lock choreography.
gaps.evidence_required: Bedrock admission packet plus terminal CI and validator evidence.
gaps.forbidden_shortcut: Do not interpret standing candidate authorization as Bedrock readiness.
gaps.accepted_risk_allowed_reason: Not eligible because Bedrock readiness gates future real execution surfaces.

gaps.user_impact: Future product mutation remains unsafe while the adjacent Project_ARIS workspace is dirty and sensitive paths are unresolved.
gaps.destination_reason: Crisol must stage the first mutation-capable gate only after workspace hygiene and scope boundaries are explicit.
gaps.closure_criteria: Establish a clean mutation boundary or explicit hygiene repair plan before any product-side change is attempted.
gaps.evidence_required: Crisol workspace hygiene check with scoped mutation plan and sensitive-path review.
gaps.forbidden_shortcut: Do not ignore workspace dirtiness just because candidate-only governance passes.
gaps.accepted_risk_allowed_reason: Not eligible because unsafe mutation boundaries would affect future remediation accuracy.

gaps.user_impact: The next destination is known, but the handoff is not yet promoted into an admitted Crisol gate.
gaps.destination_reason: A separate Crisol gate must decide whether the future runtime and real-evidence scope is admissible.
gaps.closure_criteria: Create a dedicated Crisol admission gate with explicit scope, locks, evidence requirements and CI confirmation.
gaps.evidence_required: Crisol admission packet with operator-approved scope and terminal CI.
gaps.forbidden_shortcut: Do not treat handoff documentation as Crisol admission.
gaps.accepted_risk_allowed_reason: Not eligible because destination admission is still pending.
